title: Gift Expands Iran Project’s Cutting-Edge Research
summary: The Belfer Center’s Iran Project has received funding from the Universal Muslim Association of America, allowing for cutting-edge research on Iran and its role in world affairs.
body: The Belfer Center’s Iran Project has received funding from the Universal Muslim Association of America, a non-governmental organization that works on social, political, religious, and humanitarian issues. This funding will allow the Iran Project to further its cutting-edge research on Iranian politics and its role in the larger Middle East and world affairs, said Iran Project Director Payam Mohseni . The gift will assist the project in launching a research initiative on the future of Shi’ism, sectarianism, and conflict resolution in the Middle East. “The primary aim of this initiative is to bring together leading voices from across Shi’a and Sunni communities and to explore strategies for sectarian de-escalation in the Middle East,” Mohseni said. The goal of the Iran Project is to increase understanding of Iran and contemporary Iranian affairs, from its nuclear program to Iranian state and society more broadly. The project also supports the efforts of Iranian students and those involved in Iranian studies at Harvard University and aims to become a diplomatic bridge to engage Iran and Iranian universities and research institutes. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Belfer Center's Iran Project has received funding from the Universal Muslim Association of America, a non-governmental organization. This gift will enable the project to expand its advanced research on Iranian politics and its regional and global role. According to Project Director Payam Mohseni, the funding will specifically support the launch of a new research initiative focused on the future of Shi'ism, sectarianism, and conflict resolution in the Middle East. The primary aim is to convene leading voices from both Shi'a and Sunni communities to explore strategies for sectarian de-escalation. The broader goal of the Iran Project is to enhance understanding of Iran, covering topics from its nuclear program to its broader state and societal dynamics. It also supports Iranian students and scholars at Harvard and aims to act as a diplomatic bridge to engage with Iran and its academic institutions.
result.main_content_zh: 贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心的伊朗项目获得了美国环球穆斯林协会的资助，该协会是一个专注于社会、政治、宗教和人道主义议题的非政府组织。这笔赠款将使伊朗项目能够进一步推进其在伊朗政治及其在中东乃至全球事务中角色的前沿研究。项目主任Payam Mohseni表示，这笔资金将特别用于启动一项关于什叶派的未来、中东教派主义与冲突解决的新研究计划。该计划的主要目标是汇聚来自什叶派和逊尼派社区的主要声音，共同探索中东地区教派紧张局势的降级策略。伊朗项目的总体目标是增进对伊朗及当代伊朗事务的理解，涵盖其核计划到更广泛的国家与社会层面。该项目还支持哈佛大学的伊朗学生及伊朗研究学者，并旨在成为一个外交桥梁，以接触伊朗及其大学和研究机构。
